steps.title: Separate the time shift
steps.content: The first sentence explains how Spacewar! challenged gamers to defeat other players. The next idea begins with "Before Spacewar!'s development" and describes an earlier, more solitary gaming experience. These should be separate sentences.

steps.title: Reject fused structures
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice between complete ideas. Choice B joins them with "and" but leaves the time phrase awkwardly attached. Choice D provides no punctuation between "players" and "before," creating a run-on.

steps.title: Use a period before before
steps.content: A period after "players" correctly ends the first sentence and starts the contrast about the earlier period. The answer is C.
